2606.07812.title: Scaling Participation in Modular AI Systems
2606.07812.paper_summary: Humanity is a mosaic of multifaceted talents and needs, and any truly intelligent AI must reflect that richness. Yet the LLMs used by all are built by the few -- a centralized market of monolithic AI models structurally ill-suited to capture the diversity of human knowledge, reasoning, and values. Here we introduce scaling participation, a new paradigm in which modular AI systems are built from the bottom up through the contributions of diverse stakeholders. Participants contribute small models trained on their own interests and priorities; these models then collaborate in modular frameworks as compositional AI systems. Participatory AI systems outperform monolithic LLMs by up to 15.4% across 15 tasks, such as reasoning and factuality, surpassing models larger than all contributed components combined. Further experiments show that participatory AI systems benefit from contributor diversity, substantially improve on each contributor's original priorities, and exhibit emergent capabilities that allow them to solve over 15% of problems where all individual models fail. Scaling participation provides a technical foundation for transitioning from the monolithic status quo toward an open, bottom-up, and collaborative AI future.
2606.07812.paper_summary_zh: 人類是一個多面向才能和需求的馬賽克，而任何真正智能的人工智慧必須反映這種豐富性。<lb/>然而，所有人使用的LLM是由少數人構建的——這是一個集中化的單一人工智慧模型市場，結構上不適合捕捉人類知識、推理和價值的多樣性。<lb/>在這裡，我們介紹了擴展參與，這是一種新範式，其中模組化人工智慧系統是通過多元利益相關者的貢獻自下而上構建的。<lb/>參與者貢獻基於他們自身興趣和優先事項訓練的小模型；這些模型然後在模組化框架中作為組合人工智慧系統進行協作。<lb/>參與式人工智慧系統在15項任務中，如推理和事實性，表現超過單一LLM多達15.4%，超越了所有貢獻組件總和更大的模型。<lb/>進一步的實驗顯示，參與式人工智慧系統受益於貢獻者的多樣性，顯著改善每個貢獻者的原始優先事項，並展現出新興能力，使它們能夠解決超過15%的問題，而所有單獨模型均失敗。<lb/>擴展參與為從單一現狀過渡到開放、自下而上和協作的人工智慧未來提供了技術基礎。
2606.07812.author: Shangbin Feng et.al.
2606.07812.authors: Shangbin Feng, Yike Wang, Weijia Shi, Luke Zettlemoyer, Yejin Choi, Yulia Tsvetkov
